examples.name: 1. an exact path
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 2. a wildcard segment (*)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/*/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 3. everything below (**)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: 4. capture a segment, check a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: 5. the whole model in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The whole model in one tree.
  # capture_encoded binds the project id
  # raw, plain or encoded. Its child
  # greedy_except admits the rest but
  # carves out a "secret" subtree.
  # allow_encoded opts in the slash.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
              greedy_except(literal("secret", greedy())))),
      allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: bare root
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The bare root matches only the
  # request "/".
  - paths:
      - /
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: multiple exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health
      - /api/v4/status
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/status
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: nested literal chain
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # literal() splits its text on "/".
  # This nested chain is the same matcher
  # as literal("api/v4/buildinfo").
  - where: |-
      path.match(
        literal(
          "api",
          literal(
            "v4",
            literal(
              "buildinfo"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/buildinfo
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: trailing slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A trailing slash is significant: this
  # matches /api/v4/health/ but not
  # /api/v4/health.
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/health/
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health/
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy **
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: single segment *
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/*/health
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/health
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: multiple methods
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - GET
      - HEAD
examples.input:
request:
    method: HEAD
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: wrong method denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/**
    methods:
      - GET
examples.input:
request:
    method: POST
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: checked against a set
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /data/*/{letter}/**
    where: contains(set("a", "b", "c"), vars.letter)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /data/x/b/info
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: checked against a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: equals identity
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/user/{username}
    where: user.name == vars.username
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/user/alice
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: optional: an optional trailing subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The path may end at files, or carry
  # the optional reports tail, from one
  # tree with no duplicated prefix.
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          optional(literal("reports"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: optional: match with or without a trailing slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          optional(slash())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: slash: a trailing slash is required
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: path.match(literal("files", slash()))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: slash: the bare root path /
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: path.match(slash())
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: additive list (two rules)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /public/**
  - paths:
      - /static/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /static/css/app.css
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: anti-pattern (commented out)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
      # - /api/status
      #   {project} not bound for this path
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: carve-out (negation)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /**
    where: '!path.match(literal("admin", greedy()))'
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/users
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: carve-out and the strict tokenizer
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A negated carve-out denies
  # files/secret. Reaching it with an
  # encoded "s" (%73) never decodes: the
  # tokenizer rejects the escape, so the
  # path is invalid before the rule runs.
  - where: |-
      !path.match(literal("files",
          literal("secret", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/%73ecret/key
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: path, method, and trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /teams/{team}/**
    methods:
      - GET
    where: contains(user.traits["teams"], vars.team)
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /teams/security/dashboards
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        teams:
            - security
            - devs

examples.name: sugared carve-out (paths + where)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # Allow all of /files, then carve out the
  # /files/secret subtree with a negated
  # path.match in where. paths set the
  # territory, where removes a slice.
  - paths:
      - /files/**
    where: |-
      !path.match(literal("files",
          literal("secret", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/a
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: glob_without: exclude single segments
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          glob_without(set("private", "secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/report
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: glob_without: one segment only, deeper is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          glob_without(set("private", "secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/a/b
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: a capture inside is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_except(capture("name"))))

examples.name: greedy_except: add greedy() to exclude the whole subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_except(literal("secret", greedy()))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/key
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_except: exact segment, subtree still allowed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_except(literal("secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/notes
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_without: an encoded path can't slip the deny
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # greedy_without grants files/** but
  # denies the secret and private
  # subtrees. Reaching secret with an
  # encoded "s" (%73) can't slip it: the
  # tokenizer rejects the escape, so the
  # path is invalid before the rule runs.
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/%73ecret/key
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_without: exclude subtrees (string sugar)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/reports/q3
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: greedy_without: the carved subtree is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/secret/key
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: a path under no root is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(root(
          literal("api", greedy()),
          literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /public/x
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: root: nested anywhere but the top is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(literal("files",
          root(literal("a"), literal("b"))))

examples.name: root: several first segments in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |-
      path.match(root(
          literal("api", greedy()),
          literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /admin/settings
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: an encoded id, a carved-out subtree, and the encoded-slash opt-in
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The whole model in one tree.
  # capture_encoded binds the project id
  # raw, plain or encoded. Its child
  # greedy_except admits the rest but
  # carves out a "secret" subtree.
  # allow_encoded opts in the slash.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
              greedy_except(literal("secret", greedy())))),
      allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: a double-encoded slash is invalid, even under an encoded-slash node
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # capture_encoded admits the encoded
  # separator, but a double-encoded slash
  # (%252F) begins with %25, not the
  # separator, so the tokenizer rejects
  # the whole path before any matcher.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"), greedy())), allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/a%252Fb/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: encoded_literal pins one group/project by its decoded value
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # encoded_literal pins one id by its
  # decoded value, in either hex case
  # (%2F or %2f). The "/" is content, so
  # the node stays one segment.
  # allow_encoded opts in the slash.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          encoded_literal("mygroup/myproject", set("/"),
              literal("issues"))), allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2Fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: an encoded node without allow_encoded fails closed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # capture_encoded names an encoded
  # position, but the match admits no
  # encoding until allow_encoded opts it
  # in. Without it an encoded path fails
  # closed; a plain segment still matches.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("registry",
          capture_encoded("package", set("/"))))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /registry/@babel%2fcore
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: GitLab files route, encoded id and encoded file path
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The files API takes an encoded id and
  # an encoded file path. Each encoded
  # position is named, and binds raw and
  # forwards byte-faithful. allow_encoded
  # opts in the slash.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"),
              literal("repository/files",
                  capture_encoded("file", set("/"))))), allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2Fmyproject/repository/files/lib%2Fsrv%2Fapp.go
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: GitLab project id, plain or encoded, in one node
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A GitLab project id is a numeric id
  # (123) or a URL-encoded group/project.
  # capture_encoded binds both forms as
  # one raw segment, no OR-doubling.
  # allow_encoded opts in the slash.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"), greedy())), allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/123/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: a broad ** never spans an encoded slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # greedy is safe-only: it spans only
  # segments with no encoding. /files/**
  # matches a plain tail but stops at an
  # encoded slash, so it never admits one.
  # The path is valid, it just matches no
  # rule.
  - paths:
      - /files/**
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /files/a/b/c
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: constrain an encoded id with a where check on the raw value
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # An encoded segment is an opaque blob:
  # the matcher can't reach inside it. An
  # inner constraint goes in a where check
  # on the raw captured value, not the
  # path tree.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"), greedy())), allow_encoded(set("/"))) &&
      vars.project == "mygroup%2Fmyproject"
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/mygroup%2Fmyproject/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: npm scoped package keeps its literal "@" raw
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # npm scoped package: /@scope%2fname,
  # as the npm registry addresses it.
  # capture_encoded binds the id raw, so
  # the "@" forwards as "@", never %40.
  # allow_encoded opts in the slash.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("registry",
          capture_encoded("package", set("/"))), allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /registry/@babel%2fcore
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: a ".." smuggled between encoded slashes is rejected
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The decode-for-validation view turns
  # a%2F..%2Fadmin into a/../admin, so the
  # ".." is caught like a raw one. The
  # path is invalid before any matcher.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("api/v4/projects",
          capture_encoded("project", set("/"), greedy())), allow_encoded(set("/")))
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/a%2F..%2Fadmin/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer

examples.name: allow code and reason
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: User has access to project
    deny_hint:
      - deny_code: denied_project
        deny_reason: User has NO access to project
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: deny hint: path matched, where failed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: User has access to project
    deny_hint:
      # No on: defaults to the path+method
      # clause. Fires when the path matched
      # but the where failed.
      - deny_code: denied_project
        deny_reason: User has NO access to project
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
    name: alice
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha
            - beta

examples.name: deny hints across rules, only some fire
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/projects/{project}/**
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    deny_hint:
      - deny_code: denied_project
        deny_reason: Not allowed on this project
  - paths:
      - /api/v4/admin/**
    where: contains(user.roles, "admin")
    deny_hint:
      - deny_code: denied_admin
        deny_reason: Admin role required
examples.input:
request:
    method: GET
    path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
    name: bob
    roles:
        - developer
    traits:
        allowed_projects:
            - alpha

examples.name: no matching role
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
# The user must hold this role for
# its app_resources to apply.
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/health"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [auditor]
